structural_evidence.scope: health-passing revalidation of default-off expired-factor projection under direct one-to-one rendered feedback
structural_evidence.promotion_status: not promoted: health passes and visual/SE3 support over-drains to 1022/1015, but no expired factors are projected on the re-run and trajectory regresses versus the accepted preset; the remaining blocker is deterministic rendered-feedback ownership/timing, not another scalar weight

structural_evidence.scope: full-window expired-factor projection without callback-time visual ingestion
structural_evidence.promotion_status: not promoted: removing callback-time visual ingestion preserves pose drain but underfeeds visual/SE3 support at 1007/1015 and regresses RMSE/path drift

structural_evidence.scope: default-off rendered-stamp ownership for visual/SE3 sliding-window factors
structural_evidence.promotion_status: diagnostic only: proves rendered-stamp factor ownership is wired through launch, status, and report fields without changing the accepted production preset

structural_evidence.scope: full-window rendered-stamp ownership for visual/SE3 sliding-window factors
structural_evidence.promotion_status: not promoted: rendered-stamp ownership improves aggregate RMSE, but misses strict pose and visual/SE3 drains, regresses path drift, and leaves late-bin support underfed; the next fix must be deterministic visual-feedback scheduling, not a scalar parameter change

structural_evidence.scope: default-off adaptive sliding-window state retention from mapper-render feedback backlog
structural_evidence.promotion_status: diagnostic only: proves adaptive retention can eliminate short-window time-expired visual/SE3 drops, but it must pass full-window throughput and trajectory gates before promotion

structural_evidence.scope: full-window adaptive sliding-window state-retention evidence under direct one-to-one rendered feedback
structural_evidence.promotion_status: not promoted: full-window adaptive retention removes time-expired drops, but retaining a 42-state active BA window collapses replay throughput to 118/1186 poses and 29/1015 visual/SE3 factors; the next structural target is asynchronous/global rendered-feedback coupling rather than growing the active window

rejected_evidence.reason: full-window dense-prior bias sub-block diagnostic keeps trajectory close to the accepted preset and final bias-prior min singular values at 6.691/6.691, but misses the production visual/SE3 drain at 926/1015 with late bins 6/7 down to 96/90 factors

full-window rendered-delivery diagnostic keeps trajectory close to the accepted preset, but misses post-play drain at 1185/1186 poses and 895/1015 visual/SE3 factors; mapper produced 1075 rendered previews while tracker received 880, with a worst-bin produced-minus-received gap of 127

direct one-to-one visual pairing eliminates duplicate pairs and keeps rendered-size mismatches at zero, but misses the production visual/SE3 drain at 890/1015, leaves a 177-frame produced-minus-received rendered gap, regresses path drift versus the accepted preset, and records one invalid LiDAR frame

callback-time visual factor ingestion closes the one-cycle executor delay for short windows and improves the depth64 full run slightly, but still misses the production visual/SE3 drain at 949/1015; split counters show 57/57 drops are time-expired with zero queue-trim drops, so the remaining blocker is out-of-sequence mapper-render feedback after active-window state expiry

adaptive retention eliminates time-expired visual/SE3 pending drops by expanding the active BA window to 42 states, but the larger active solve collapses full-window throughput to 118/1186 poses and only 29/1015 visual/SE3 factors, so it is a diagnostic that rules out active-window growth as the production fix

expired-factor projection closes the visual/SE3 drain and passes health on revalidation, but the replay consumes 1022/1015 factors with zero actual expired projections and regresses RMSE/path drift; production needs deterministic rendered-feedback ownership before this path can be promoted
turning off callback-time ingestion while projecting expired visual factors reaches the pose drain, but only drains 1007/1015 visual/SE3 factors and regresses both RMSE and path drift

rendered-stamp visual-factor ownership improves aggregate RMSE, but misses the strict pose drain by one, drains only 959/1015 visual/SE3 factors, records 37/37 stale visual/SE3 drops, and regresses path drift versus the accepted production preset

initial continuous-time visual/SE3 support interpolation relied on numeric Jacobian blocks, underfed the full-window visual/SE3 stream at 591/1015, recorded one invalid LiDAR frame, and regressed RMSE
bulk cache backfill reaches 1085/1015 visual and SE3 factors, but creates 43 visual-alignment pending stale drops and regresses RMSE
skipping saturated 2D visual alignment on reconciled pairs improves path drift and keeps 1107 SE3 factors, but underfeeds visual alignment at 761/1015 and still regresses RMSE
local photometric fallback restores visual/SE3 support to 1083/1015 and lowers drift, but misses one pose drain and still regresses RMSE versus the accepted production preset
same-direction search/photometric agreement reaches 1252/1015 visual factors and improves path drift, but overfeeds the window and regresses RMSE

observed-stamp deduplication proves duplicate observed-image consumption was real with 636 rejected duplicates, but it drains only 959/1015 visual and 960/1015 SE3 factors and regresses RMSE/path drift

one-to-one observed/rendered pairing eliminates duplicate visual pairs and reaches the pose drain, but it drains only 966/1015 visual and 972/1015 SE3 factors, leaves bin 7 at 68/68, and regresses RMSE/path drift versus the accepted production preset
deferring one-to-one reconciliation to point-cloud callbacks improves aggregate RMSE, but misses the 983/1015 visual and 986/1015 SE3 drains, leaves bin 7 at 51/51, and regresses path drift

deferring direct visual-pair processing to point-cloud callbacks eliminates duplicate visual pairs, but misses the 912/1015 visual and 919/1015 SE3 drains, leaves bin 7 at 57/59, and regresses RMSE/path drift

combining point-cloud deferred visual-pair processing with rendered-image QoS depth 64 restores rendered delivery to 943/946, but misses the 790/1015 visual and 794/1015 SE3 drains, records 179/179 pending stale drops, and regresses RMSE/path drift

moving point-cloud deferred visual-pair reconciliation before same-cycle visual-factor consumption closes the executor one-cycle lag, but still misses the 946/1015 visual and 952/1015 SE3 drains, records 53/53 pending stale drops and one invalid LiDAR frame, and regresses RMSE versus the accepted production preset
path drift improves, but RMSE regresses from the production preset
late-only release still regresses RMSE
later release still regresses RMSE
stronger visual/SE3 weights regress RMSE
wider 2D visual search radius misses the visual/SE3 drain target and regresses RMSE/path drift
ZNCC lowers path drift but misses the visual/SE3 drain target and regresses RMSE
saturated-only photometric-step visual factor source misses the visual/SE3 drain target and regresses RMSE
full 64-bit visual source ids lower path drift but miss the visual/SE3 drain target and regress RMSE
legacy source-id pair counters show zero duplicate visual pairs, but the run still misses visual/SE3 drain and regresses RMSE/path drift
mapper-continuity replay shows best-effort mapper image input received only 900/1187 frames, produced 899 renders, missed visual/SE3 drain, and regressed RMSE/path drift
dual-endpoint reliable mapper image QoS produces 1019 renders with only two image drops, but tracker visual/rendered pairing still misses the 1015 visual/SE3 drain target at 718 and regresses RMSE
rendered-image QoS depth 64 nearly restores visual/SE3 drain at 1008/1015 but overfeeds pending visual factors, drops 50 queued factors, and regresses RMSE/path drift
larger pending visual-factor queue does not fix BA/window ingestion; it still misses visual/SE3 drain at 881/1015, drops 150 queued factors, and regresses RMSE/path drift
quality-ranked visual/SE3 selection with max 2 factors per reference fixes early queue churn but misses the 1015 visual/SE3 drain target at 956, drops 44 queued factors, and regresses RMSE/path drift
raising quality-ranked selection to max 3 reaches total visual/SE3 drain at 1018 but still starves bin 7, drops 61 queued factors, and regresses RMSE/path drift
late-only quality-ranked selection after 100 s still misses the 1015 visual/SE3 drain target at 994, starves bins 6/7, drops 64 queued factors, and regresses RMSE/path drift
disabling the per-reference quality cap improves path drift but still misses pose and visual/SE3 drains, starves bins 6/7, drops 78 queued factors, and regresses aggregate RMSE

delaying no-reference-cap quality trimming to 108 s nearly preserves aggregate RMSE but still misses pose and visual/SE3 drains, starves bins 6/7, drops 94 visual and 79 SE3 pending factors, and slightly regresses path drift
quality weighting preserves the no-trim diagnostic path and lowers path drift, but it weakens visual/SE3 constraints enough to miss the drain target at 934 and regress aggregate RMSE
current defaults keep the diagnostic quality paths disabled and preserve trajectory-shape drift, but this replay does not reproduce the accepted visual/SE3 drain and reaches only 912/1015 factors

nearest-sample mapper synchronization lowers path drift but misses the visual/SE3 drain at 928/1015, records one invalid LiDAR frame, and regresses aggregate RMSE versus the accepted strict FIFO synchronizer

directly applying upstream Coco-LIC gyro/accel random-walk sigmas keeps coverage and factor continuity but over-constrains the ROS2 bias residual units, shortening the current path to 18.46 m vs 33.35 m reference

distance-only motion-length factors are live and keep path drift near the accepted preset, but they underfeed visual/SE3 continuity at 930/1015, introduce one invalid LiDAR frame, and regress aggregate RMSE
late-only pre-BA agreement release is live and improves final-bin/path-length shape, but it underfeeds visual/SE3 continuity at 794/1015 and moves the worst RMSE into bins 5/6
